name: Samak Curry (Curried Fish)
recipeIngredient: 2 celery stalks with leaves, finely diced
3 fillets orange, cut in half (about 1 3/4 lbs), or any firm white fish
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the dried lime in half and remove seeds. Pound the lime in a mortar or puree it in a blender until finely ground. You will need about 1 heaping teaspoon.
2. Heat olive oil in a skillet that can hold all the fish fillets in one layer. Sauté carrot, celery, onions, and garlic until softened.
3. Add curry powder, salt, pepper, nutmeg, ground lime, lemon zest, parsley, and coriander to the skillet. Stir well.
4. Add about 1/2 cup water to thin out the paste if needed.
5. Arrange the fish fillets in a single layer in the skillet. Cover and cook for about 5 minutes on each side or until cooked through.
6. Serve immediately with rice. Makes 6 servings. Per serving: 154 calories, 23 grams protein, 8 grams carbohydrates, 3 grams fat, 0 grams saturated fat, 65 milligrams cholesterol, 103 milligrams sodium.
